name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 11.05.2026

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 11.05.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 11.05.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on May 08, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 83.08 lakh ha as compared to 80.01 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on May 08, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 83.08 lakh ha as compared to 80.01 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 08.05.2026, 96.36% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. 100% area of Pulses has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 98.02% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (99.66%), 65.70% area of Rice and 98.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 08.05.2026, 96.36% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. 100% area of Pulses has been completed; 99.87% area of Oilseeds majorly in Rapeseed & Mustard (100%); 98.02% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (99.66%), 65.70% area of Rice and 98.00% area of Wheat has been harvested for rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in many  parts  of  Northwest India, Gujarat state, Konkan-Goa, coastal-Karnataka and Northeast India.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in many  parts  of  Northwest India, Gujarat state, Konkan-Goa, coastal-Karnataka and Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  10  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  6% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 12%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 3%; (iii) Central India: 41%; and South Peninsula: (-) 9%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  10  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  6% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 12%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 3%; (iii) Central India: 41%; and South Peninsula: (-) 9%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A low-pressure area is likely to form over southwest Bay of Bengal during the next 48 hours.
texts.text:  A low-pressure area is likely to form over southwest Bay of Bengal during the next 48 hours.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated thunderstorms, lightning & gusty winds likely over northwest India during 10th -14th May, 2026. Isolated hailstorm activity also likely over Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh on 11th & 12th; Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand on 12th & 13th May.
texts.text:  Isolated thunderstorms, lightning & gusty winds likely over northwest India during 10th -14th May, 2026. Isolated hailstorm activity also likely over Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh on 11th & 12th; Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand on 12th & 13th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Arunachal Pradesh and Assam & Meghalaya, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and Kerala & Mahe.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Arunachal Pradesh and Assam & Meghalaya, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal and Kerala & Mahe.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave conditions at isolated places likely over Jammu & Kashmir on 10th; West Rajasthan, Gujarat during 10th -14th; East Rajasthan during 10th -13th; West Madhya Pradesh during 12th -14th May, 2026.
texts.text:  Heat wave conditions at isolated places likely over Jammu & Kashmir on 10th; West Rajasthan, Gujarat during 10th -14th; East Rajasthan during 10th -13th; West Madhya Pradesh during 12th -14th May, 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  07  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 66.830 billion cubic meter, which is 36.41% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.10 percent of last  year's  storage  and  124.84  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  07  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 66.830 billion cubic meter, which is 36.41% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 114.10 percent of last  year's  storage  and  124.84  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Litchi  (Stinkbug  and  Mealy  bug)  in  Bihar.  The  Advisory  has  been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic  Threshold  Level  for  Litchi  (Stinkbug  and  Mealy  bug)  in  Bihar.  The  Advisory  has  been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During  the  week  (30  April  -  06  May  2026),  higher  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 79%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 06 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 8%.
texts.text:  During  the  week  (30  April  -  06  May  2026),  higher  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 79%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 06 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 8%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during many days of the week.
texts.text: Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be above normal over the southern, eastern, and northeastern parts of the country;  below  normal  over  the  Western  Himalayan  region  and  near  normal  over  the  rest  of  the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be above normal over the southern, eastern, and northeastern parts of the country;  below  normal  over  the  Western  Himalayan  region  and  near  normal  over  the  rest  of  the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (08  May - 14 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (08  May - 14 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 3-4°C likely over Northwest India till 10th May and no significant change during 11th -13th May.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 3-4°C likely over Northwest India till 10th May and no significant change during 11th -13th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Central India till 08th May and a gradual rise by 2-4°C thereafter.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Central India till 08th May and a gradual rise by 2-4°C thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  3-5°C  likely  over  Northeast  India  till  08th  and  no significant change during 10th -13th May.
texts.text:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  3-5°C  likely  over  Northeast  India  till  08th  and  no significant change during 10th -13th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat  wave  conditions  likely  to  prevail  in  isolated  pockets  over  Gujarat  state  and  West  Rajasthan during 09th -13th, 2026.
texts.text:  Heat  wave  conditions  likely  to  prevail  in  isolated  pockets  over  Gujarat  state  and  West  Rajasthan during 09th -13th, 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (15  May - 21 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (15  May - 21 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low Probability of Heatwave conditions likely during some days of week 2 over Rajasthan, Gujarat, Vidarbha, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh. Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over parts of Rajasthan & adjoining Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat; above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) temperatures are likely along the northwest, central & adjoining eastern parts of the country. Near-normal maximum temperatures are likely across rest of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Low Probability of Heatwave conditions likely during some days of week 2 over Rajasthan, Gujarat, Vidarbha, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh. Appreciably above normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over parts of Rajasthan & adjoining Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat; above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) temperatures are likely along the northwest, central & adjoining eastern parts of the country. Near-normal maximum temperatures are likely across rest of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.
texts.text:  No warm night conditions are likely during the week.

texts.text: 3. CROPS

texts.orig: Rabi Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Rabi Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses : About 24.97 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 23.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses : About 24.97 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 23.76 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.orig: As on 07 May, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 66.830 BCM (36.41%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
